name: override-precedent
description: The trust loop: an approved precedent in memory flips the verdict from escalate to auto_merge.

pr.title: Round checkout totals half-up instead of half-even
pr.author: deval
pr.description: Switches checkout total rounding from banker's rounding (half-even) to half-up to match finance's documented expectation.

files.src/checkout/pricing.ts: export const roundTotal = (n: number): number => Math.round(n * 100) / 100;

trustLoop.query: checkout total rounding half-up half-even pricing precedent decision

precedent.text: PR #311 (askable/shop): Align rounding with finance spec. Verdicts: semantic-review=warn. Decision: approve_merge — Finance confirmed half-up is the documented, intended rounding for checkout totals; this pattern is approved.

metadata.reason: Finance confirmed half-up rounding is the intended, documented behaviour for checkout totals.
metadata.author: human-reviewer

response.summary: The PR changes checkout total rounding, which diverges from the half-even behaviour documented in the code. Without a prior decision on record this looks like a behavioural regression.

findings.title: Unreviewed change to money rounding
findings.detail: roundTotal switches from half-even to half-up rounding for checkout totals, changing customer-facing amounts. No precedent justifies the change.

response.intentSummary: Change checkout total rounding to half-up.
unmetIntent: Rounding direction diverges from the documented half-even behaviour with no recorded justification.

response.summary: An approved precedent (askable/shop#311) confirms half-up is the documented, intended rounding for checkout totals. This change matches that approved decision.
response.intentSummary: Change checkout total rounding to half-up, matching an approved precedent.
